summary: Implemented an MVP combined messaging/social app with Internet Identity auth, user profiles, global feed, Discord-style servers/channels, 1:1 DMs, polling-based near-real-time updates, and shared media uploads rendered across posts and messages.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication + minimal user profile
existing_features.description: Users sign in with Internet Identity and complete a minimal profile (display name, optional avatar reference) used across posts, servers/channels, and DMs.

existing_features.title: Global feed with post creation and browsing
existing_features.description: Authenticated users can create posts with text and optional attachments; all users can browse the feed in reverse chronological order.

existing_features.title: Servers and channels (create + browse channel timelines)
existing_features.description: Authenticated users can create servers and text channels; users can view a per-channel message timeline and post messages.

existing_features.title: Direct messages (1:1) with threads and timelines
existing_features.description: Authenticated users can start 1:1 conversations (by principal string) and exchange messages in a DM thread timeline.

existing_features.title: Polling-based near-real-time refresh for feed, channels, and DMs
existing_features.description: Client polling keeps the global feed, channel timelines, and DM threads refreshed close to real time without sockets.

existing_features.title: Shared media uploads for posts and messages (blob storage)
existing_features.description: Frontend supports uploading attachments; backend stores blobs and returns references rendered back as post/message attachments.

existing_features.title: Core navigation and screens: Feed, Servers, DMs, Profile/Settings
existing_features.description: Coherent navigation and core pages to move between global feed, servers/channels, direct messages, and profile/settings.

existing_features.title: Cohesive modern UI theme with consistent components and spacing
existing_features.description: Applied a distinct theme and component styling across feed and messaging layouts (avoiding a blue/purple primary palette).

existing_features.title: Generated brand/UX images included as public assets and used in UI
existing_features.description: Generated and placed static assets under frontend/public/assets/generated and rendered them in relevant UI locations (logo/empty states).

existing_features.title: Scoped-down MVP implementation to unblock initial failed build
existing_features.description: Delivered a smaller-scoped but complete end-to-end MVP including auth, feed, servers/channels, DMs, polling, and media uploads.

existing_features.title: Auth gate/redirect protection for authenticated-only pages (e.g., Servers)
existing_features.description: Unauthenticated users are blocked from accessing authenticated sections (notably Servers) via a frontend AuthGate that prompts/redirects to Internet Identity sign-in.

existing_features.title: Username autocomplete/resolution for user targeting (DM start and related inputs)
existing_features.description: UI supports searching/autocompleting usernames to resolve a target user/principal for actions like initiating DMs (and reusable user selection inputs).

existing_features.title: Quick profile preview popover from user identity UI
existing_features.description: Users can open a compact profile preview popover when interacting with a user’s name/avatar in messaging and similar contexts.

existing_features.title: Profile avatar handling improvements (upload flow and avatar components)
existing_features.description: Profile avatar upload/rendering flow was updated, including dedicated AvatarUploader/UserAvatar components, to improve reliability of setting and displaying avatars.

existing_features.title: Remove any public display of user Principal IDs across the entire UI (including public profiles, profile popovers, post/message author areas, server lists, DM lists, and any other user surfaces). The app must never show another user’s Principal ID anywhere in the UI.

In the signed-in user’s Profile & Settings page, add a user-only control to reveal/hide their own Principal ID (hidden by default on load), and include a copy-to-clipboard action. Do not show the Principal ID on this page unless the user explicitly chooses to reveal it.

Update the Profile & Settings UI to allow the signed-in user to edit bio and manage per-field public/private visibility toggles for all supported profile fields. All user-facing text for these controls must be in English.

Update all profile display surfaces (including the profile popover and any public profile UI) to show meaningful public profile information (e.g., display name, avatar, bio, join date when public) rather than showing only a Principal ID. Do not display non-public fields to other users.

Fix server management controls visibility so that server edit/delete (and related management) actions appear for the actual server owner. Ensure ownership checks are based on the server owner Principal and the frontend permission checks use the owner Principal returned by the backend.

Enhance @mention UX by formatting mentions in rendered post/message text and making mentions clickable to open a user profile surface (e.g., profile popover). Mentions must resolve to the correct user identity (Principal) for profile lookup, without exposing Principal IDs in the UI.

new_feature_requests.summary: Profile image: let users choose between uploading an image or providing an image URL
Global feed post creation: allow adding an image URL (in addition to current attachments)
Channel/DM messaging: allow users to delete their own messages
Server channel post/message creation: let users choose between uploading an image or providing an image URL
Post deletion permissions: users/server owners can delete their own posts; server owners can delete others’ posts
Servers and channels management: support editing and deleting servers and channels (with appropriate permissions)
Servers/channels customization: support configuring server + channel details beyond basic edit/delete (e.g., descriptions/topics, icons/banners, settings)
Profile page enhancements: add more configurable profile fields and/or profile settings sections beyond the current minimal profile
Introduce a server role/rank permission system to control who can edit/delete/customize servers and channels
Profile/Settings: add a toggle to control visibility of the user’s ID (principal)
Add username-based typing/autocomplete/resolution in relevant places for easier access (beyond DM initiation)
Privacy: hide users’ principal IDs everywhere by default; only reveal in own Profile via an explicit user action (e.g., “Show my ID” button)
Profiles: show more than principal ID on user profiles (display name/avatar and other basic public profile fields)
@mention UX: make mentions richer (e.g., autocomplete picker, highlight/format, and/or clickable mentions to open profile)
Add a notification center/popup surface (placeholder for future integrations like mentions/activity)
Profiles: allow users to control visibility (public/private) of additional profile fields (e.g., bio, join date, etc.) beyond display name/avatar
Profiles: add bio + join date fields and allow users to toggle public/private visibility for all profile fields (including display name/avatar/bio/join date)
Privacy: remove principal ID display from all public UI surfaces; allow viewing principal only in the signed-in user’s own Profile/Settings via an explicit show/hide button
Remove Principal ID display from all public UI surfaces (profiles, popovers, post/message author areas, server lists, etc.). The app must never show another user’s Principal ID anywhere in the UI.

In the signed-in user’s Profile & Settings page, replace any always-visible Principal ID text with an explicit user-only show/hide control (e.g., “Show my ID” / “Hide my ID”), and include a copy-to-clipboard action. The Principal must be hidden by default on page load.

Extend backend user profiles to support additional fields (at minimum: bio and join date) and per-field public/private visibility controls for all profile fields (display name, avatar, bio, join date, and any other stored profile fields). Public profile queries must only return fields marked public to non-owners, while the profile owner must be able to fetch and edit all fields and visibility settings.

Update the Profile & Settings UI to allow the signed-in user to edit bio and manage per-field visibility toggles for all supported profile fields (display name, avatar, bio, join date, and any other stored fields). Ensure user-facing text is in English.

Update profile display surfaces (e.g., profile popover and any public profile UI) to show meaningful public profile information (display name, avatar, bio, join date when public) instead of showing only a Principal ID. Do not display non-public fields to other users.

Fix server management controls visibility so that edit/delete actions appear for the actual server owner. Ensure ownership checks are based on the server owner Principal (not display name), and wire the frontend to use that owner Principal for permission checks.

Enhance @mention UX beyond autocomplete by formatting mentions in rendered message/post text and making them clickable to open the mentioned user’s profile surface (e.g., a profile popover). Mentions must resolve to the correct user identity (Principal) for profile lookup.

Add any required conditional state migration to preserve existing user profiles when upgrading the backend profile schema (e.g., initialize new fields and visibility settings with safe defaults, and set join date for existing users).

Extend backend user profiles to support additional fields (at minimum: bio and join date) and per-field public/private visibility controls for all profile fields (including display name, avatar, bio, join date, and any other stored profile fields). Public profile queries must only return fields marked public to non-owners, while the profile owner must be able to fetch and edit all fields and visibility settings.

Add any required conditional state migration to preserve existing user profiles when upgrading the backend profile schema, initializing new fields and visibility settings with safe defaults and setting join date for existing users where appropriate.

architectural_notes: Confirmed initial scope: near-real-time messaging; mix of Discord-style servers/channels + global feed; support text plus images/uploads from v1.
Backend uses Motoko canister modules for authorization/access control and blob storage, with frontend using an actor-based client.
Near-real-time UX is implemented via client polling intervals for feed/channel/DM timelines (no realtime sockets).
Frontend is a React + Tailwind + shadcn/ui component system with a shared AppLayout/AppNav and dedicated pages for Feed/Servers/DMs/Profile.
Add URL-based image references as an alternative to uploaded blobs for profile images and post attachments.
Introduce delete/edit permission rules for messages, posts, servers, and channels (ownership + server-owner moderation).
Add a username search/index path to resolve a username to principal for initiating DMs.
Upcoming scope: add richer server/channel configuration & customization, plus expanded Profile page settings.
Planned permissions expansion: add role/rank system (RBAC) for server/channel management beyond simple ownership checks.
Profile settings should include a user-controlled privacy toggle for showing/hiding the user’s principal/ID in the UI.
known_issues: Public profiles currently show only the user’s principal ID (and expose principal publicly), instead of showing basic public profile fields like display name/avatar.
Server management controls (settings/edit/delete) are still not appearing in the UI for authorized users, blocking server management.
Profile image upload is not working reliably for users (needs investigation/fix).

generatedImages.description: Minimalist chat-and-feed logo mark (flat vector, warm neutral palette).
Empty state illustration for a sparse social feed (flat vector).
Empty state illustration for a quiet chat/messages view (flat vector).
Header banner brand image for the app UI.

projectName: All-in-one messaging + social app (servers/channels, global feed, private chats) with media uploads
